modules.title: Classification of signals and systems
topics: Continuous‑time (CT) signals: step, ramp, pulse, impulse, sinusoidal and exponential signals
Discrete‑time (DT) signals: step, ramp, pulse, impulse, sinusoidal and exponential signals
Basic operations on signals (addition, scaling, time‑shifting, time‑reversal, etc.)
Classification of signals: periodic vs. aperiodic, energy vs. power signals, random signals
CT and DT systems and their basic properties (causality, stability, linearity, time‑invariance)
Linear time‑invariant (LTI) systems and their fundamental properties

modules.title: Analysis of continuous‑time signals
topics: Fourier series analysis and spectrum of periodic CT signals
Laplace transform and region of convergence (ROC)

modules.title: Linear time‑invariant continuous‑time systems
topics: Differential‑equation representation of LTI CT systems
State‑variable representation and matrix form of CT systems
Impulse response and step response of CT systems
Frequency response (Bode plot, magnitude‑phase) of CT systems
Design and analysis of analog filters

modules.title: Analysis of discrete‑time signals
topics: Convolution sum for DT signals and its properties
Sampling of CT signals, Nyquist criterion and aliasing
Discrete‑time Fourier transform (DTFT) and its properties
Z‑transform of DT signals, poles, zeros and ROC

modules.title: Linear time‑invariant discrete‑time systems
topics: Difference‑equation representation of LTI DT systems
Analysis of DT LTI systems using DTFT and Z‑transform
State‑variable equations and matrix representation for DT systems
Design and implementation of digital filters

reference_books: Alan V. Oppenheim, Alan S. Willsky, S. Hamid Nawab, ‘Signals and Systems’, 2nd edition, Pearson New International Edition, 2015
A. Anand Kumar, Signals and Systems, PHI, 3rd edition, 2015
Mahmood Nahvi, Signals and Systems, McGraw‑Hill
Simon Haykin and Barry Van Veen, Signals and Systems, Wiley India
A. Nagoor Kani, Signals and Systems, McGraw‑Hill
Robert A. Gabel and Richard A. Roberts, Signals & Linear Systems, Wiley
Rodger E. Ziemer, William H. Tranter, D. Ronald Fannin, Signals & Systems, Pearson Education

modules.title: Introduction, history and overview of measurement system; fundamentals of measurement system; static and dynamic characteristics of measurement systems; systematic characteristics; generalized model; transfer function; techniques for dynamic compensation; accuracy of measurement systems in steady state; measurement error; error probability function; error reduction techniques; reliability; choice and economics of measurement systems; loading effects due to shunt‑connected and series‑connected instruments; calibration curve; testing & calibration of instruments
topics: Introduction, history and overview of measurement systems
Static and dynamic characteristics of measurement systems
Transfer function and techniques for dynamic compensation
Accuracy in steady state – measurement error and error probability function
Error reduction techniques, reliability, choice and economics of measurement systems
Loading effects caused by shunt‑connected and series‑connected instruments
Calibration curve and procedures for testing & calibration of instruments

modules.title: Galvanometers – theory, principle of operation and construction
topics: Definition and classification of analog and digital instruments
Operating principles of analog instruments, operating force, types of supports, damping and controlling mechanisms

modules.title: Different types of ammeters and voltmeters; digital instruments
topics: PMMC, moving iron (MI), electrodynamometer and induction ammeters & voltmeters
Expression for control and deflection torque, advantages, disadvantages and error sources
Extension of range using shunt and multiplier techniques
Digital voltmeter, digital ammeter, multimeter and wattmeter

modules.title: Instrument transformers
topics: Ratio and phase‑angle errors; testing procedures for instrument transformers
Differences between CT and PT; error reduction techniques

modules.title: Measurement of power
topics: Power measurement in AC and DC circuits
Electrodynamometer type wattmeter – construction, theory, operation and errors
Three‑phase power measurement – one‑, two‑ and three‑wattmeter methods
Reactive power measurement by single wattmeter method
Power measurement using current and potential transformers

modules.title: Measurement of energy
topics: Single‑phase and three‑phase digital/electronic energy meters – construction and operation
Energy flow and power calculations; error analysis
Tri‑vector meter, maximum demand meter and ampere‑hour meter

modules.title: Power factor meter
topics: Single‑phase and three‑phase electrodynamometer type power factor meters

modules.title: Resistance measurement
topics: Classification of low, medium and high resistance

modules.title: Magnetic measurement
topics: Power loss measurement in sheet metal using Lloyd‑Fischer square

experiments: Measurement of low resistance using Kelvin’s double bridge
Measurement of medium resistance using Wheatstone’s bridge
Measurement of high resistance by loss of charge method
Measurement of insulation resistance using Megger
Measurement of earth resistance by fall of potential method and verification by using earth tester
Measurement of power in a single‑phase AC circuit by 3‑voltmeter/3‑ammeter method
Calibration of a dynamometer type wattmeter with respect to a standard/sub‑standard wattmeter
Calibration of single‑phase digital/electronic type energy meter
Calibration of a dynamometer type wattmeter by phantom loading method
Measurements using instrument transformers
Study of various types of indicating instruments
Measurement of power in three‑phase circuit by one, two & three wattmeter methods
reference_books: A.K. Sawhney; ‘A course in Electrical & Electronic Measurements & Instrumentation’; Dhanpat Rai & Co. (P) Ltd., New Delhi
G. K. Banerjee; ‘Electrical and Electronic Measurements’. PHI Learning Pvt. Ltd.
R. B. Northrop; ‘Introduction to Instrumentation and Measurement’; CRC Press, Taylor & Francis
Vijay Singh; ‘Fundamentals of Electrical & Electronic Measurements’, New Age International

modules.title: Circuit Elements and Basic Analysis
topics: Introduction to circuit elements R, L, C and their characteristics (linearity, time dependence)
Voltage and current sources (controlled and uncontrolled)
Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) and Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL) analysis

modules.title: Transient and Steady‑State Analysis
topics: Transient analysis in RL, RC, and RLC circuits
Steady‑state analysis using phasors and vectors

modules.title: Network Topology and Graph Theory
topics: Network graph, trees, branches and links
Incidence matrix, cut‑set and tie‑set matrices

modules.title: Network Theorems
topics: Analysis with dependent and independent sources

modules.title: Frequency Domain and Laplace Transform
topics: Laplace transform solution of integro‑differential equations
Transform of step, ramp, gate and sinusoidal waveforms
Network theorems in the transform domain

modules.title: Fourier Series and Signal Spectra
topics: Signal spectra and Fourier series coefficients

modules.title: Two‑Port Networks and Network Functions
topics: Complex frequency and network/transfer functions for one‑port and two‑port networks
Two‑port parameters Z, Y, ABCD, hybrid and image parameters
Interconnection of two‑port networks and terminated two‑port networks

experiments: To Verify Thevenin Theorem.
To Verify Superposition Theorem.
To Verify Reciprocity Theorem.
To Verify Maximum Power Transfer Theorem.
To Verify Millman’s Theorem.
To Determine Open Circuit parameters of a Two Port Network and to Determine Short Circuit parameters of a Two Port Network.
To Determine A, B, C, D parameters of a Two Port Network.
To Determine h parameters of a Two Port Network.
To Find Frequency Response of RLC Series Circuit.
To Find Frequency Response of RLC parallel Circuit.

William H. Hayt & Jack E. Kemmerly, Steven M. Durbin; Engineering Circuit Analysis; McGraw‑Hill
Richard C. Dorf, James A. Svoboda, Introduction to Electric Circuits, Wiley India, 2015
Charles K. Alexander & Matthew N.O. Sadiku: Electrical Circuits; McGraw‑Hill
J. David Irwin, Robert M. Nelms, Engineering Circuit Analysis, Wiley India, 2015
Robert L. Boylestad, Introductory Circuit Analysis, Pearson, 2016
M. S. Sukhija, T. K. Nagsarkar; Circuits and Networks, Oxford University Press, 2015
Samarajit Ghosh, Network Theory Analysis and Synthesis

modules.title: Semiconductor Diodes
topics: Theory of P‑N junction, temperature dependence and breakdown characteristics
Zener diode, Varactor diode, Tunnel diode, PIN diode, LED, Photo diode, Schottky diode
Diode applications: series–parallel configurations, full‑wave and half‑wave rectification, voltage multiplier circuits, diode testing

modules.title: Transistors
topics: BJT types and configurations, working principle, characteristics, and regions of operation
Load line analysis and biasing methods
Small‑signal analysis of transistor (low frequency) using h‑parameters, thermal runaway and thermal stability
Transistor as an amplifier – gain, bandwidth, frequency response

modules.title: Feedback Amplifier and Oscillators
topics: Feedback amplifier concepts, negative feedback, voltage‑series, voltage‑shunt, current‑series and current‑shunt feedback
Sinusoidal oscillators: L‑C (Hartley‑Colpitts) oscillators, RC phase‑shift, Wien bridge, crystal oscillators
Power amplifiers: class A, class B, class AB, class C, their efficiency and power dissipation
Push‑pull and complementary symmetry push‑pull amplifier configurations

modules.title: Wave Shaping Circuits
topics: Switching characteristics of diode and transistor: turn‑ON, turn‑OFF time, reverse recovery time, transistor as switch
Differential amplifier – calculation of differential and common‑mode gain, CMRR using h‑parameters

modules.title: Operational Amplifier
topics: Operational amplifier fundamentals, practical op‑amp circuits and characteristics
Slew rate, bandwidth, offset voltage, bias current
Summing, averaging, differentiator, integrator, differential, instrumentation, log and antilog amplifiers
Voltage‑to‑current and current‑to‑voltage converters, comparators, Schmitt trigger
Active filters, 555 timer and its applications

experiments: Design & measure the frequency response of an RC coupled amplifier using discrete components.
Design a two‑stage RC coupled amplifier and determine the effect of cascading on gain and bandwidth.
Study the effect of voltage‑series, current‑series, voltage‑shunt and current‑shunt feedback on amplifier using discrete components.
Design & realize inverting, non‑inverting and buffer amplifier using 741 op‑amp.
Verify the operation of a differentiator circuit using op‑amp IC 741 and show that it acts as a high‑pass filter.
Verify the operation of an integrator circuit using op‑amp 741 and show that it acts as a low‑pass filter.
Design & verify the operation of adder and subtractor circuit using op‑amp 741.
Plot frequency response of AC coupled amplifier using op‑amp 741 and study the effect of negative feedback on the bandwidth and gain of the amplifier.
Study of IC 555 as astable and monostable multivibrator.
Design & realize using op‑amp 741, Wien‑bridge oscillator.
reference_books: Robert L. Boylestad, Louis Nashelsky; Electronic Devices and Circuits; Pearson.
Jacob Millman, Cristos C. Halkias, Satyabrata Jit; Electronic Devices and Circuits; McGraw‑Hill.
Anil K. Maini; Electronic Devices and Circuits; Wiley.
S. Salivahanan, N. Suresh Kumar; Electronic Devices and Circuits; McGraw‑Hill.

Class Priority Queue and Interface Queue
